rulings.Case Title: ENKBAATAR TSEDENDORJ VS. VLADIMIR YEVTUSHENKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 2, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT JANNET NGO, KENNY VOONG AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion is off calendar. First, the moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Second, moving party failed to provide 75-days notice in compliance with CCP 437c. See McMahon v. Sup. Crt. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112; Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1268. Moving party's remedy is to seek a trial continuance in Department 206 if they wish to re-calendar the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 2, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC., A CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted as to the seventh cause of action against Cellular Gopher, and denied as to the remaining causes of action against Cellular Gopher and Mr. Yum. Tian Shan Trading (USA), Inc. has not met its initial burden of proof against Mr. Yum as to either cause of action. For the first cause of action, Cellular Gopher has demonstrated a triable issue of fact whether it rejected the goods. For the seventh cause of action, the failure of Cellular Gopher to update its address with the Secretary of State does not create a triable issue of fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: DAIYING ZHANG VS. ALBERT SHUI-CHUN CHENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 2, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT DANIEL SULLIVAN,, JUANITA SULLIVAN,, CARL OLSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION The Court grants the motion for summary judgment. Defendant did not owe a duty of care to plaintiff. The evidence shows that plaintiff's misuse of the skylight was unforeseeable, the skylight did not pose a dangerous or defective condition on the premises, there were no previous injuries on the roof due to the skylight, and it is undisputed that plaintiff's general practice was to prohibit persons from accessing the roof. Plaintiff's authorities are inapposite because they involve the situation where the landowner invited the injured plaintiff onto the premises and in this case, plaintiff was trespassing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL UNITED METHODIST CHURCH, A VS. OVERAA/MILLER CONSTRUCTION, et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 2, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT OVERAA/MILLER CONSTRUCTION, A JOINT VENTURE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION The motion for summary adjudication is granted. Plaintiff's complaint implicates RHI's work and RHI owes an immediate duty to defend Overra pursuant to § 11 of the subcontract as a matter of law. See Crawford v. Weather Shield (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541. rulings.Case Title: WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY VS. KEN PAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement By Deft Kenneth Page; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 2, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT KENNETH PAGE Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement By Deft Kenneth Page; Request For Judicial Notice The motion is denied without prejudice. Defendant Page fails to present substantial evidence showing that the settlement was in the ballpark. See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Defendant needs to present evidence showing a rough approximation of plaintiff's recovery and the defendants' proportionate liability. See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n.6. The Court rejects Chicago Title's other arguments. The stay is lifted for the limited purpose of hearing this motion and Chicago Title is a joint tortfeasor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: QING LIU MO VS. LILLIAN MOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have First Set Of Admissions Deemed Admitted By Defts Lu Mock;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 2, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF QING MO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have First Set Of Admissions Deemed Admitted By Defts Lu Mock; Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT
